title: echinocactus polycephalus subsp. xeranthemoides
intro: Echinocactus polycephalus subsp. xeranthemoides, also known as the hedgehog prickly pear cactus, is a type of cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat skin irritations and infections.
